records.ety: [F. orgie, orgies, L. orgia, pl., Gr. <?/; akin to <?/ work. See Organ, and Work.]

defs: A sacrifice accompanied by certain ceremonies in honor of some pagan deity; especially, the ceremonies observed by the Greeks and Romans in the worship of Dionysus, or Bacchus, which were characterized by wild and dissolute revelry. Usually in the plural form.
quotes: As when, with crowned cups, unto the Elian god, Those priests high orgies held.
notes: [The singular is rarely used in this sense.]

defs: Wild, drunken, or licentious revelry; an uninhibited carouse.
A series of sexual activities involving more than two couples in a group.
An event characterized by unrestrained indulgence in passion; as, an orgy of buying of internet stocks.
A frantic revel; drunken revelry. See Orgies
